title: Free the States Statement on Pro-Life Bills to Be Heard in the 2/21/22 OK Senate HHS Committee Hearing
author: James Silberman
orientation: A Free the States memo delivered to Republican members of the Oklahoma Senate HHS Committee ahead of a 2/21/22 hearing, urging 'no' votes on five incremental pro-life bills (SB1552, SB1553, SB1555, SB1503, and SJR37) authored chiefly by Senator Greg Treat and Senator Julie Daniels. It frames each bill as virtue signaling, Roe-deferential, or actively weakening Oklahoma's existing constitutional protections, and points readers toward true abolition bills SB1372 and HB4111.
audiences: Oklahoma state legislator (especially Republican committee member)
Pro-life voter or activist deciding between incrementalism and abolition
Christian pastor or church leader weighing political compromise

questions.question: Which five bills did the Oklahoma Senate HHS Committee plan to hear on February 21, 2022, and who authored them?
questions.answer: Senate Bills 1552, 1553, 1555, 1503, and Senate Joint Resolution 37. Four of the five were authored by Senator Greg Treat, with the fifth authored by Senator Julie Daniels.
questions.quote: Four of the five incremental pro-life bills being heard this afternoon have been authored by Senator Greg Treat, the man most responsible for preventing the abolition of abortion in Oklahoma since 2019. The fifth bill was authored by Senator Julie Daniels.

questions.question: Why does Free the States oppose SB1552, the bill aimed at helping mothers in need?
questions.audience: Pro-life voter or activist deciding between incrementalism and abolition
questions.answer: While helping mothers is good, FTS argues that using such a bill to posture as a pro-life champion while refusing to actually stop abortion is monstrously evil and amounts to virtue signaling that will not save lives.
questions.quote: It is good to help mothers in need, but it is monstrously evil to use bills like this one to posture oneself as a pro-life champion while refusing to actually stop the bloodshed.

questions.question: What makes SB1553 worse than the trigger ban already on Oklahoma's books?
questions.audience: Oklahoma state legislator (especially Republican committee member)
questions.answer: SB1553 functions as an additional trigger bill that only takes effect if Roe is overturned, but it is weaker than the existing 2021 trigger because it permits abortion up to 30 days of gestation.
questions.quote: All SB1553 does is add an even weaker trigger to the one already on the books in Oklahoma.

questions.question: What is Free the States' theological and constitutional objection to SB1555?
questions.audience: Christian pastor or church leader weighing political compromise
questions.answer: By tying enforcement to the Attorney General certifying that Roe is overturned, SB1555 perpetuates the legal fiction that state officials must defer to the Supreme Court rather than obey Christ and uphold their oaths to the Constitution.
questions.quote: Each governing official is first to obey Christ, and then to uphold your oath to the Constitution. You can obey the Law of God and uphold your oath of office, or you can obey the unconstitutional, genocidal pro-abortion jurisprudence of the Supreme Court. You cannot do both.

questions.question: How does Free the States argue that SJR37 would actually weaken Oklahoma's constitutional protection of life?
questions.answer: Article II, Section II-2 of the Oklahoma Constitution already guarantees the inherent right to life; SJR37 would shift the state from constitutionally prohibiting abortion to being constitutionally neutral on it.
questions.quote: SJR37 would actually weaken our Constitutional protection of life by taking Oklahoma from a place of Constitutionally prohibiting abortion to being Constitutionally neutral on abortion.

questions.question: What specific carve-outs in SB1503 does Free the States call out as disqualifying?
questions.audience: Oklahoma state legislator (especially Republican committee member)
questions.answer: SB1503 dehumanizes preborn children without detectable heartbeats, bars governing officials from enforcing the act, and exempts abortions performed at the behest of federal agencies, contractors, or employees.
questions.quote: Further, SB1503 establishes that the prohibitions contained in “[s]ections 3 and 4 of this act do not apply to an abortion performed at the behest of federal agencies, contractors, or employees.” Vote no on SB1503.

questions.question: What does Free the States want legislators to do instead of passing these incremental bills?
questions.answer: FTS urges legislators to coauthor SB1372, which has been significantly revised from SB495, and HB4111 as the path that obeys God, upholds their oaths, and actually defends preborn children.
questions.quote: If you are serious about obeying the Law of God, upholding your oath, and defending preborn children, coauthor SB1372 (which has changed in three significant ways from SB495) and HB4111.

questions.question: Why does the article predict another self-congratulatory press release from Oklahoma legislators?
questions.answer: Last year, on the very day legislators killed the abolition bill and signed what FTS calls a death warrant for 5,000 preborn children, they released a statement celebrating themselves; FTS expects the same political theater after this hearing.
questions.quote: Last year, the day they killed the abolition bill and passed six pro-life incremental bills in its place, they sent out a press release about how great a day for preborn children it was, despite it being the very day that the death warrant for 5,000 preborn children was signed.

related.reason: Companion essay to this memo from January 2022 that develops the moral case against the incremental approach embodied in SB1552-SB1555.

related.title: Incrementalism Versus Immediatism in a Nutshell
related.reason: Concise primer on the two positions, useful for readers new to why FTS rejects bills that 'allow murder up to 30 days.'
